_provenance.stage: Stage 6 — Synthesis (retroactive rebuild from Codex Stage 3)
version_history: v3 (2026-05-17): retroactive editorial pass. Stage 3 (Codex) FAS=68 with 4 verified claims. Most important correction: 'removed judicial review' (MISLEADING — overstates the effect of Section 15B) replaced with the accurate 'bars review of discretionary acts except on procedural-compliance grounds'. Article 149 framing corrected to specify the named articles it overrides (5, 9, 10, 13, 79). 'On minister's advice' (UNVERIFIED) dropped from Board-appointment description. 'Disproportionately drawn from lower-income communities' (UNVERIFIED — Stage 3 said this requires denominator-based socioeconomic data) replaced with SUHAKAM/Bar Council recorded-complaints framing. View card editorialized as policy proposal sourced to SUHAKAM and Bar Council rather than stated as requirement.

revision_log: CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Headline 'No Judicial Review' overstated Section 15B effect. Replaced with 'Section 15B Bars Merits Review by Court' (accurate: courts retain procedural-compliance jurisdiction).
CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Hook 'removed judicial review' → 'Section 15B insulates discretionary decisions from court review on merits'.
CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Fact 1 'POCA 2014 amendment removed judicial review' → 'Section 15B bars review of Board discretion; courts retain jurisdiction over procedural-compliance grounds'.
REMOVED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'on the minister's advice' (Section 7B states Board appointed by the Yang di-Pertuan Agong; ministerial-advice qualification not in the section).
REMOVED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'No public reasoning standard' dropped; Stage 3 said the Act provides for Inquiry Officer reports and Board decisions.
CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Fact 2 sub on Article 149 sharpened to list specific articles overridden (5, 9, 10, 13, 79).

CORRECTED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — Fact 3 'disproportionately drawn from lower-income communities' replaced with SUHAKAM/Bar Council recorded-complaints framing; transparency gap argument kept but framed as the absence of denominators.

REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Reframe 'active, routine detention regime' softened to 'operationally active preventive-detention regime sitting outside a courtroom' (avoids overstating routine character).

REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Analogy 'prison sentence by an appointed committee' (overstates equivalence) → 'registered-person watchlist decided by an appointed committee, with detention as the operating consequence'. Article 151 advisory-board safeguard acknowledged in sub.
REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — View 'POCA reform requires either...' editorialized as 'SUHAKAM and Bar Council have urged...' (sourced to named reform proposals).

PRESERVED (Stage 3 VERIFIED): POCA 1959 + 2014 amendment; 2-year renewable orders; 60-day police investigation + 24h+21d+38d structure; Prevention Board hearing; Article 5 personal liberty; Article 149 carve-out.
